findings.message: Duplicate data-access layer: importing from item_service (add_item, fetch_all_items) creates a second data-access abstraction layer. The repository module already provides equivalent functions (list_items, create_item). This violates the layering principle and creates maintenance burden with code duplication.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate to a single data-access layer. Either migrate all code to use repository.py (renaming functions if needed for clarity) or migrate seed.py to use item_service.py. Ensure consistent query patterns and transaction handling across the codebase.

findings.message: Transaction handling split between service layer and route handler: item_service.add_item() only flushes (line 15 of item_service.py), but the route handler calls session.commit() and session.refresh() after. This couples the service layer contract to the caller and creates inconsistent transaction semantics compared to repository.create_item() which handles its own commit.
findings.suggestion: Have the service layer own the complete transaction: add_item() should handle both flush and commit, returning a fully-persisted object. The route handler should only call the service and return the result, not manage transactions.
